meta.title: Jaguar AJ20P6 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost

hero.tagPill: Jaguar • AJ20P6 / P400 • Petrol • 3.0L I6 Turbo • 2019–present
hero.h1: Jaguar AJ20P6 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
hero.subHeadline: The Jaguar AJ20P6 is a modern 3.0‑litre straight‑six petrol — strong and refined, but cooling system monitoring is essential. Backed by 80+ real UK enquiries in 2025 [EM‑VERIFIED].

rows.metric: Confidence: 85% — based on 80 UK enquiries and early‑market specialist feedback.

rows.value: A mix of manageable cooling issues and more complex timing‑chain work

rows.metric: Available but not yet as ubiquitous as older engines; JLR‑specific parts

rows.value: Reconditioned cost against lowest host value (~£30,000) is well under 30%

rows.metric: Sum of the five rows above
rows.value: One-line verdict: The AJ20P6 is a capable, modern six‑cylinder — but early examples need coolant system vigilance — buy with a full JLR service history.

rows.metric: Best for: Drivers wanting a smooth, powerful petrol SUV or saloon with reasonable running costs.
rows.value: Avoid if: Buyers expecting the simplicity of older naturally‑aspirated sixes.
Also powers the Land Rover Range Rover, Velar and Defender → [link].
Labour estimate: Fitting an AJ20P6 typically takes 12–16 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [EM‑QUOTE].

rows.metric: Shared‑cost note: Parts cost is identical across Jaguar and Land Rover applications; labour time varies slightly by engine bay access.
rows.value: → Compare AJ20P6 engine prices from UK specialists
How much does a Jaguar AJ20P6 engine replacement cost?

rows.metric: A reconditioned AJ20P6 typically costs £4,500–£7,500 supply only [EM‑QUOTE], varying by specification and power output. Fitting adds £780–£1,040 depending on labour rates.
rows.value: Is the Jaguar AJ20P6 a reliable engine?

rows.metric: The AJ20P6 scores 3.5/5 for reliability [JAG‑VERIFIED], with 85% confidence based on early‑market data. Cooling system maintenance is the key to longevity.
rows.value: What is the most common AJ20P6 failure?

rows.metric: The most common issue is coolant system leaks — from water pumps, expansion tanks or plastic fittings — typically appearing between 60,000–120,000 miles.
rows.value: Which Jaguar models use the AJ20P6?

rows.metric: The AJ20P6 powers the Jaguar F‑Pace P400 and XF P400 — the brand's premium petrol SUV and executive saloon.
rows.value: Is the Jaguar AJ20P6 the same as Land Rover's version?

rows.metric: Yes — it's the same 3.0‑litre straight‑six Ingenium petrol engine, used across both brands with identical parts and shared cooling‑system characteristics.
rows.value: Why UK AJ20P6 Owners Trust jaguarengine.uk

rows.metric: * Every garage and engine rebuilder in our network is vetted, rated and reviewed by real AJ20P6 owners, as part of the Engine Finders network.
rows.value: * We compare real companies, not just lead‑gen sites — every specialist on EnginesMarket has genuine UK workshop premises and verified modern Jaguar experience.

rows.metric: * Our data is sourced from real UK AJ20P6 enquiries, combined with direct interviews with Jaguar specialists across the country.
rows.value: Find the best Jaguar AJ20P6 engine replacement UK drivers rely on. Compare real prices from trusted specialists on EnginesMarket today and get back on the road with confidence.

rows.metric: CTA Button: Compare Jaguar AJ20P6 Engine Prices Now →
rows.value: Internal note: Step 0 mode = Shared (AJ20P6 also fitted to Land Rover models). Cross‑link to Land Rover equivalent page to be added once built.
